Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to analyze a provided event log from a manufacturing assembly line The log contains raw sensor data temperature pressure vibration flow tool position energy without explicit activity labels I need to use process mining techniques pattern recognition clustering based on sensor values to infer the underlying activities and label them accordingly

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Data I need to look at the table row by row and identify clusters or transitions in the sensor values

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Event 1 3 Temp 20 Pressure 1 Vibration 0 Flow 0 Tool Pos 0 Energy 0 45 Observation Everything is at baseline zero except energy is slightly above zero

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This looks like an Idle or Standby state

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Event 4 6 Temp rises 50 to 58 Pressure rises 1 to 1 5 Vibration spikes 30 to 38 Flow starts 5 Tool Pos moves 5 to 10 Energy increases 2 0 to 3 0 Observation High activity Tool is moving Vibration is significant Material is flowing

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This looks like a machining operation Cutting or Machining fits well here The tool position is changing suggesting movement across the material

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: Event 7 8 Temp drops 40 Pressure rises 2 Vibration drops 10 Flow drops 2 Tool Pos moves 15 Energy stable 2 0 Observation Tool is still moving but vibration and flow are lower Pressure is higher

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: This feels like a transition or a different phase of machining perhaps Drilling or Finishing

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: However looking at Event 9 there is a huge spike Let us look at Event 9

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Event 9 Temp spikes 80 Pressure 2 Vibration spikes 50 Flow 0 Tool Pos 15 same as prev Energy spikes 5 0 Observation High energy high temp high vibration no material flow Tool position is static at 15

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This looks like a high intensity stationary process Welding or Heat Treatment fits well Given the context of assembly Welding is a strong candidate

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Event 10 12 Temp drops 30 to 25 Pressure 1 Vibration low 1 to 5 Flow low 0 to 1 Tool Pos moves 15 to 20 Energy drops 1 0 to 1 6 Observation Cooling down Tool moves to 20 Very low vibration and flow

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: This looks like Inspection or Post Processing or simply Retracting Resetting

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Given the low flow and low energy compared to the cutting phase but tool movement it might be Quality Inspection or Packaging However usually inspection happens before packaging Let us look at the flow

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Event 13 15 Back to baseline Temp 20 Vibration 0 Flow 0 Pos 0 Observation Reset to start

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize Phases Activities Phase 1 Events 1 3 Idle Setup Phase 2 Events 4 6 Active Machining High vibration moving tool material flow Cutting Metal Phase 3 Events 7 8 Transition Secondary Machining

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Could be part of the cutting phase or a specific Finishing step

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Let us group 4 8 together as one main activity block or split

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Let us consider 4 8 as Machining Cutting Wait Event 9 is distinct

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Pressure goes up vibration goes down This could be Assembling press fit But Tool Position is moving It is likely one continuous operation

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Let us simplify into broader categories for the Process Mining exercise Idle Standby Events 1 3 Cutting Machining Events 4 8 Welding Joining Event 9 Inspection Finishing Events 10 12 Reset Events 13 15

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait is Reset an activity In process mining Idle is often a state not a process step I should focus on the value adding steps

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Let us group the log into a process flow Start Idle 1 3 Cutting 4 8 Welding 9 Inspection Quality Check 10 12 End Reset 13 15

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Rationale Check Cutting 4 6 High vibration 30 38 high temp 50 58 material flow 5 Tool moves Classic machining Welding 9 Temp 80 highest Energy 5 0 highest Vibration 50 highest Tool static at 15 No flow This is clearly a thermal process Inspection 10 12 Temp drops 30 to 25 Energy drops Tool moves to 20 Flow is 1 maybe air Idle baseline

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Decision I will group 4 8 as Machining Cutting because the tool position is continuous 5 to 15 The change in pressure vibration might just be depth of cut or feed rate change

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait one detail Event 10 12 Tool Position is 20 Event 1 3 Tool Position is 0 So after Inspection the machine resets to 0 This confirms 10 12 is the end of the cycle before reset

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final check on Event 7 8 Event 8 and 9 are both at position 15 This implies 8 is the end of the cutting phase holding at the cut location 9 is the welding phase at that location So 4 8 is definitely one phase Machining 9 is Welding 10 12 is moving away Inspection Handling

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This makes a logical manufacturing sequence Prep Cut Join Inspect Reset
